beats.directive: Decompose into Q1 (parallel systems overlap?), Q2 (concept tier across 11+ named concepts), Q3 (layout-vs-conceptual mismatch?), Q4 (where in code does a newcomer hit a wall?), Q5 (rename/merge/delete vs redesign per item?).

In: tools/symphony/, tools/meta-score/, .github/agents/maestro*, top-level READMEs. Out: tools/plugins/** (deferred to refactor template). Audit shape: terminology+structure+layering, not runtime behavior.
Per concept x system: type def, top 3 call sites, prompt/fixture mentions. Repo memory may suggest anchors but every claim in steps 5-9 must cite a code site, not memory.
At each anchor, capture (suggests / does / gap) with file+line. Floor: >=1 cited site per sub-question; >=2 sites for any relational claim.
Group concepts by load-bearing tier (load-bearing / derived / accidental) x system overlap (meta-score-only / maestro-only / symphony-only / shared). Tier assignment requires code-site citation.
Test H-a (parallel systems dominant), H-b (vocabulary dominant), H-c (layout dominant) with counter-evidence. Each hypothesis cites code (>=2 for relational).
Per concept: keep-as-is / rename / merge / delete / unresolved. Unresolved gets needs-investigation tag and does NOT enter the refactor queue.
Answer all 5 sub-questions. Name dominant axis explicitly: terminology | control-flow | layout | layering. If dominant != terminology/layout, halt and flag wrong-template.

Emit refactor target list: <=12 targets, ranked by (confusion-severity x cite-count). Each target: imperative title + scope (consumable by one refactor invocation; multi-file programs split into sequenced targets) + template tag + prerequisite IDs.
